예레미야 13:1.content: Yahweh said to me, “Go, and buy yourself a linen belt, and put it on your waist, and don’t put it in water.”

예레미야 13:2.content: So I bought a belt according to Yahweh’s word, and put it on my waist.

예레미야 13:3.content: Yahweh’s word came to me the second time, saying,

예레미야 13:4.content: “Take the belt that you have bought, which is on your waist, and arise, go to the Euphrates, and hide it there in a cleft of the rock.”

예레미야 13:5.content: So I went and hid it by the Euphrates, as Yahweh commanded me.

예레미야 13:6.content: After many days, Yahweh said to me, “Arise, go to the Euphrates, and take the belt from there, which I commanded you to hide there.”

예레미야 13:7.content: Then I went to the Euphrates, and dug, and took the belt from the place where I had hidden it; and behold, the belt was ruined. It was profitable for nothing.

예레미야 13:8.content: Then Yahweh’s word came to me, saying,

예레미야 13:9.content: “Yahweh says, ‘In this way I, will ruin the pride of Judah, and the great pride of Jerusalem.

예레미야 13:10.content: This evil people, who refuse to hear my words, who walk in the stubbornness of their heart, and have gone after other gods to serve them, and to worship them, will even be as this belt, which is profitable for nothing.

예레미야 13:11.content: For as the belt clings to the waist of a man, so I have caused the whole house of Israel and the whole house of Judah to cling to me,’ says Yahweh; ‘that they may be to me for a people, for a name, for praise, and for glory; but they would not hear.’

예레미야 13:12.content: “Therefore you shall speak to them this word: ‘Yahweh, the God of Israel says, “Every container should be filled with wine.” ’ They will tell you, ‘Do we not certainly know that every container should be filled with wine?’

예레미야 13:13.content: Then tell them, ‘Yahweh says, “Behold, I will fill all the inhabitants of this land, even the kings who sit on David’s throne, the priests, the prophets, and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, with drunkenness.

예레미야 13:14.content: I will dash them one against another, even the fathers and the sons together,” says Yahweh: “I will not pity, spare, or have compassion, that I should not destroy them.” ’ ”

예레미야 13:15.content: Hear, and give ear. Don’t be proud, for Yahweh has spoken.

예레미야 13:16.content: Give glory to Yahweh your God, before he causes darkness, and before your feet stumble on the dark mountains, and while you look for light, he turns it into the shadow of death, and makes it deep darkness.

예레미야 13:17.content: But if you will not hear it, my soul will weep in secret for your pride. My eye will weep bitterly, and run down with tears, because Yahweh’s flock has been taken captive.

예레미야 13:18.content: Say to the king and to the queen mother, “Humble yourselves. Sit down, for your crowns have come down, even the crown of your glory.

예레미야 13:19.content: The cities of the South are shut up, and there is no one to open them. Judah is carried away captive: all of them. They are wholly carried away captive.

예레미야 13:20.content: Lift up your eyes, and see those who come from the north. Where is the flock that was given to you, your beautiful flock?

예레미야 13:21.content: What will you say, when he sets over you as head those whom you have yourself taught to be friends to you? Won’t sorrows take hold of you, as of a woman in travail?

예레미야 13:22.content: If you say in your heart, “Why have these things come on me?” Your skirts are uncovered because of the greatness of your iniquity, and your heels suffer violence.

예레미야 13:23.content: Can the Ethiopian change his skin, or the leopard his spots? Then may you also do good, who are accustomed to do evil.

예레미야 13:24.content: “Therefore I will scatter them, as the stubble that passes away, by the wind of the wilderness.

예레미야 13:25.content: This is your lot, the portion measured to you from me,” says Yahweh, “because you have forgotten me, and trusted in falsehood.”

예레미야 13:26.content: Therefore I will also uncover your skirts on your face, and your shame will appear.

예레미야 13:27.content: I have seen your abominations, even your adulteries, and your neighing, the lewdness of your prostitution, on the hills in the field. Woe to you, Jerusalem! You will not be made clean. How long will it yet be?”
